title: Certificate Authentication
text:
[REQ-0015] The agent MUST authenticate inbound peers using X.509 certificates via the TLS Transport Security Model (RFC 5953).
[REQ-0016] USM and community-string authentication MUST NOT be supported.

[REQ-0017] The agent MUST accept its own certificate and private key at construction time.

[REQ-0018] The agent MUST accept a set of trusted CA certificates at construction time, used to verify connecting peers.

[REQ-0019] The agent MUST reject any TLS handshake from a peer whose certificate does not chain to a trusted CA.

[REQ-0020] Outbound traps are sent over plain UDP and MUST NOT require any authentication or encryption.

Certificate changes require restarting the agent.

**Rationale:**
Certificate-based authentication eliminates the shared-secret weaknesses of USM and community strings.
Requiring a restart for certificate changes keeps the implementation simple; the agent is expected to be lightweight and fast to restart.
Traps are sent over plain UDP and therefore have no TLS context to authenticate.
